config._comment_connector_class: This tells Debezium to use the MySQL CDC plugin. Other plugins exist for PostgreSQL, MongoDB, etc.
config._comment_connection: Debezium connects to MySQL as a replication SLAVE using the debezium user (created in 02-exam-db.sql)
config._comment_server_id: A unique ID for this Debezium instance. Must match --server-id in MySQL config. MySQL uses this to track which replicas have received which binlog events.
config._comment_topic_prefix: All Kafka topics created by this connector will be prefixed with this value. Topic name = {prefix}.{database}.{table} = exam_db.exam_db.results
config._comment_filter: Only watch exam_db (not auth_db). Only watch the results table (not exams or saga_state — those changes don't need notifications).
config._comment_schema_history: Debezium maintains an internal schema history — it tracks DDL changes (ALTER TABLE, etc.) so it can correctly interpret binlog events even if the schema changed over time. Stored as a Kafka topic.
config._comment_decimal: By default, MySQL DECIMAL fields are encoded as base64 bytes in Debezium events. Setting 'double' makes them a regular floating-point number — much easier to work with in the bridge.
config._comment_snapshot: initial = on first start, Debezium reads ALL existing rows and publishes them as 'r' (read) events. 'schema_only' = only capture the schema, skip existing rows. 'never' = don't snapshot at all. We use 'schema_only' so we don't send emails for old data on startup.
config._comment_converters: These control how Kafka messages are serialized. JsonConverter = plain JSON (no Avro/Protobuf schema registry needed). schemas.enable=false removes the verbose JSON Schema wrapper, giving us clean flat JSON.
